verdict_reasoning: HSPA1A (HSP70-1) is canonically a cytosolic/nuclear chaperone, but it is one of the best-documented examples of stress-induced and cancer-cell-restricted surface translocation. Under heat shock, oxidative stress, hypoxia, and other proteotoxic stresses, HSP70 translocates to the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane — without a conventional TM domain — via an unknown lipid-raft-associated mechanism involving phosphatidylserine and ceramide. Cancer cells constitutively display surface HSP70 (cmHSP70) as a tumor-cell-restricted ecto-chaperone; this has been exploited by anti-HSP70 antibodies, NK-cell-based therapies, and CAR-T programs. Surface HSP70 is also displayed on apoptotic/ICD cells. Baseline localization is cytoplasmic (no stable PM pool in unstressed normal cells), but the documented cancer-ecto and stress-ecto pools are well-established and clinically targeted. Verdict: contextual / cell_state_induced.
